content.problemStatement:
The structure of the company ShareChat can be described by a simple graph (without multiedges and self-loops) with NNN vertices numbered 111 through NNN and MMM edges numbered 111 through MMM. Each vertex of this graph is part of at most one simple cycle.

For each edge, you should find the number of simple paths that contain this edge and only contain at most one edge which belongs to a cycle.
constraints: the sum of N+MN+MN+M for all test cases does not exceed 5â‹…1065 \cdot 10^65â‹…106
the sum of answers for all edges in all test cases is smaller than 150â‹…106150 \cdot 10^6150â‹…106
the sum of N+MN+MN+M for all test cases does not exceed 5â‹…1055 \cdot 10^55â‹…105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv, denoting the vertices connected by edge iii.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print MMM lines. For each iii (1â‰¤iâ‰¤M1 \le i \le M1â‰¤iâ‰¤M), the iii-th of these lines should contain a single integer â€” the number of paths for edge iii.
